subject: [PATCH v4 00/11] mm: PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed the concern that PMD swap entries are not properly handled in various architectures, explaining that they added infrastructure to detect these entries and provided generic no-op helpers for unsupported architectures.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the handling of PMD swap entries in __split_huge_pmd_locked(), specifically when splitting a PMD swap entry into PTE-level swap entries. The author added a new branch to handle this case, which splits the PMD swap entry into 512 PTE swap entries without needing a folio reference. The author also updated the folio_remove_rmap_pmd() gate to inspect old_pmd rather than *pmd. A VM_WARN_ON_ONCE(freeze) is used to catch any future callers that break the invariant of leaving page and folio uninitialized in this branch.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries in copy_huge_pmd() and copy_huge_non_present_pmd(). They modified these functions to duplicate PMD swap entries instead of splitting them, mirroring the behavior for PTE entries. The author also added retry logic for GFP_ATOMIC table allocation failures. A fix is planned.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about zswap handling large folios by teaching zswap_load() to scan the covered range and adding zswap_range_has_entry(). The patch will need further revision as the author plans to add new functionality.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries during swapoff by adding unuse_pmd() and calling it from unuse_pmd_range(). This mirrors the existing unuse_pte_range() but operates at PMD granularity. If the PMD-order folio cannot be allocated, or if the swap cache already contains per-page folios in the covered range, or if the folio is not uptodate, the PMD swap entry will be split into PTE-level entries via __split_huge_pmd() and a non-zero error will be returned.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries in various non-present PMD walkers, such as smaps_pmd_entry(), hmm_vma_handle_absent_pmd(), madvise_free_huge_pmd(), and mincore_pte_range(). The author added new helpers to handle PMD swap entries correctly, including smaps_account_swap() for accounting swap and swap_pss in the PTE and PMD paths. The changes ensure that swapped-out THPs are handled properly in these walkers.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about handling PMD swap entries in the madvise.c file, specifically when MADV_WILLNEED is used on a PMD swap entry. They explained that swapin_walk_pmd_entry() already skips non-present PMDs and therefore MADV_WILLNEED is a no-op on a PMD swap entry. The author also provided code changes to handle PMD swap entries under pmd_trans_huge_lock(), including pinning the swap device and reading the folio in at PMD order via swapin_sync().

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about move_pages_huge_pmd() failing for non-trans_huge, non-migration PMDs due to the PMD swap entry being valid but move_swap_pte() refusing swap-cache folios that are still large. The author added a new function move_swap_pmd(), modeled on move_swap_pte(), which moves the swap entry whole-PMD and re-anchors a PMD-sized swap-cache folio's anon rmap to the destination VMA, and modified the dispatcher in move_pages_huge_pmd() to route PMD swap entries through this new function.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the handling of PMD-level swap entries in do_huge_pmd_swap_page(). They added code to handle various cases, including when the swap cache still has one PMD-sized folio covering the range, and when the range has been split into per-page cache state. The author also handled zswap-backed slots and ensured that the PMD is mapped read-only when necessary. A fix is planned for v2.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: The reviewer asked for clarification on how order-0 pages within a PMD swap entry are handled, specifically whether they are compressed and stored independently in zswap during the swapout path.
reviews.raw_body:
So in the swapout path, a single PMD swap entry is created, but
order-0 pages are compressed and stored independently in zswap? Just
checking I understood this correctly.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: Reviewer Yosry Ahmed questioned the change from returning -EINVAL to -EIO in this patch, wondering if it has any practical implications or is simply a stylistic preference.
reviews.raw_body:
Just curious, why the change from -EINVAL to -EIO here? Does it matter
in practice or is it just a matter of personal taste? :)

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledged that zswap stores swapped-out THPs as independent order-0 entries keyed by individual swap slots, which causes issues on PMD-order swap-in. They explained the current behavior and how it's handled when a PMD swap entry is split.
reviews.raw_body:
Yes that is correct, swapout installs PMD swap entry in try_to_unmap_one().
When the swapped-out THP reaches zswap, current zswap still stores it as
independent order-0 entries keyed by the individual swap slots. On
PMD-order swap-in, we only proceed as a THP if the swap cache still has
one PMD-sized folio, or if none of the covered slots is present in zswap
and the whole range can be read from disk. If any slot is still backed by
zswap, we split the PMD swap entry and retry through the normal PTE path.

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: The author addressed a concern about the change in error handling semantics for zswap, explaining that -EINVAL was previously used to indicate unsupported large folios and is now replaced by -EIO to signal safe completion failure.
reviews.raw_body:
The caller only checks for != -ENOENT so no functional difference here. I viewed
it as a change in semantic, -EINVAL meant that you passed a large folio, which
wasn't supported to -EIO meaning the request is valid but zswap cannot safely
complete a PMD-sized read from per-page compressed state.

I dont want to speak for Alexandre, will let him comment on this properly :)
But above is what I thought of when I got the patch from Alex.

reviews.author: Yosry Ahmed
reviews.summary: The reviewer requested that the patch description include a brief explanation of the reasoning behind each change, specifically noting that it is not just about adding code but also about explaining the design decisions and trade-offs.
reviews.raw_body:
A mention of the reasoning in the changelog would be nice, even if
it's only semantic.

2026-07-13.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new feature to Linux kernel called PMD-level swap entries for anonymous THPs (Transparent Huge Pages). The current implementation splits huge pages into PTE-level swap entries when they are swapped out, which can lead to performance issues. This series proposes to store huge pages as single PMD-level swap entries, allowing them to be restored directly on swap-in without the need for khugepaged to collapse the range later. The patch series includes 11 patches that add support for detecting and handling PMD swap entries, including splitting them into PTE-level entries when necessary, and updating various kernel functions to handle this new type of swap entry.
